body.title: [triage] PRE-EXISTING REDS at HEAD: 4 carried failures — a carried red destroys 'a red is always a real regression'
body.intention: I intend that a red test is never carried as background noise — because a suite with a known-red member cannot tell a NEW regression from the old one, so the next real break arrives invisible.
body.description:
Found 2026-07-13 while proving the sprint-entry intention gate. VERIFIED PRE-EXISTING: reproduced in a clean git worktree at HEAD, so it is NOT a regression from that work.

FAILING:
  tests/test_budget_gate.py::TestDispatchBudgetGate::test_dispatch_checks_balance_before_or_call
  tests/test_budget_gate.py::TestDispatchBudgetGate::test_dispatch_records_spend_after_or_call

WHY IT MATTERS (and why it is not merely untidy): per CLAUDE.md the suite is meant to be green so that A RED IS ALWAYS A REAL REGRESSION. A carried red destroys exactly that property — the signal 'the suite went red' stops distinguishing 'I broke something' from 'that one was already broken', which is the same non-injective-signal defect as R-feedback-is-unconditional. It cost real time today: I had to spin a worktree at HEAD to find out whether I had caused it.

**Affected files:** tests/test_budget_gate.py, unseen_university/devices/inference/ (dispatch balance check).

**Scope boundary:** IN — diagnose whether the test or the code is wrong, and fix the one that is. OUT — redesigning the budget gate.

**Test plan:** the two named nodes go green, and the reason is understood (not silenced with a skip).

**Proof obligation:** the hollow build marks them xfail/skip and reports a green suite — which is the defect, not the fix. What a hollow build could not pass: the two nodes PASS, and a mutation to the balance check turns them red again (proving they test the balance check and not the mock).

**Completion criteria:** 1. Both nodes pass. 2. No skip/xfail added. 3. A deliberate mutation to the balance-check code reddens them.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

---
**AMENDED 2026-07-13 (while proving T-intent-extractor-crash-masquerades-as-refusal).**
TWO MORE carried reds found, both VERIFIED PRE-EXISTING in a clean worktree (HEAD~2,
before any of today's work — `git worktree`, never `git stash`):

  3. tests/intent/test_intent_extractor.py::test_store_get_db_url_fallback
     `IntentStore._DB_URL_KEYS = ("UU_HOME_DB_URL", "UU_HOME_DB_URL")` — a COPY-PASTE
     DUPLICATE. The intended fallback was `IGOR_HOME_DB_URL`, which the test still
     asserts. The error message gives it away in its own words: "set UU_HOME_DB_URL or
     UU_HOME_DB_URL". NOTE THE DECISION HIDING HERE: per CLAUDE.md, IGOR_HOME is
     ELIMINATED (T-uu-eliminate-igor-home-env) — so the TEST is asserting a retired env
     var and is probably what's wrong, not the code. That is a judgment call about a
     ratified decision, NOT a test to be quietly rewritten to green. Resolve it
     deliberately: either drop the fallback + the test, or restore the fallback.

  4. tests/igor/test_cc_queue_dispatch_is_assignment.py::TestCmdNextMarksInProgress::
     test_cmd_next_marks_ticket_in_progress — `cc_queue` has no attribute
     `TICKETS_ROOT_ID`. A symbol the test still reaches for that the module no longer
     has: stale test, or a lost constant. Determine which.

THE COST IS COMPOUNDING, AND IT IS THE SAME DEFECT AS EVERY OTHER TICKET TODAY: a
carried red makes 'the suite went red' a NON-INJECTIVE SIGNAL — it can no longer
distinguish 'I broke something' from 'that was already broken'. Today it cost TWO
worktree spins (one per failure) just to establish innocence. Each carried red raises
the price of the next honest red.
